creator: rootly.com

evidence.text: When something breaks in production, the urge to find someone to blame is immediate—almost instinctive. Yet, pointing fingers rarely solves the deeper problem . Traditional postmortems often focus on identifying who caused an outage, turning a learning opportunity into a defensive, performative meeting . But modern reliability practices demand a different approach.

title: How to Run Effective Blameless Postmortems
